item.title: A web-based prediction model using routinely available clinical variables to estimate surgical risk in Crohn's disease during remission or mild activity.
Malignancy in Long-Standing Perianal Fistulizing Crohn's Disease: Too Little, Too Late.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease (PFCD) is a chronic, aggressive phenotype associated with an elevated risk of malignancy arising within long-standing fistula tracts. Although rare, PFCD-associ...

item.title: Early immunomodulator therapy reduces the risk of intestinal resection in Crohns disease: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The optimal timing for initiating immunomodulators (IMs) in Crohns disease (CD) remains unclear. We assessed whether early IM therapy reduces the risk of intestinal resection. MEDLINE, EMBASE, and ...

item.title: Frailty in inflammatory bowel disease: a missing link in personalized care for the aging IBD population.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Frailty is a potentially reversible condition that reflects decreased physiological reserve and is increasingly recognized in older patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Although frailty ...

item.title: Effect of polydeoxyribonucleotide as a potential therapeutic agent to enhance the healing of Crohn's perianal fistula.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The treatment of Crohn's perianal fistula remains challenging owing to its recurrent nature and resistance to complete healing. This study aimed to assess the postoperative outcomes, including fist...

item.title: Decreasing primary resection rates for Crohn's disease but unchanged re-resection rates: A nationwide Danish population-based cohort study from 1997 to 2021.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - The overarching goal in the medical management of Crohn's disease (CD) is to minimize disease burden for patients and prevent disease progression, where surgery often is necessary. The aim of this ...

item.title: Less Common Vulvar Dermatologic Diseases.
item.description: Clinical obstetrics and gynecology - Less common vulvar dermatologic disorders discussed here can mimic infection, inflammatory dermatoses, and malignancy. This review outlines 4 entities clinicians should recognize. A pyogenic granul...

item.title: Lack of disparities in postoperative care after ileocecal resection in patients with Crohn's disease at tertiary inflammatory bowel diseases centers.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Prior studies suggest that longer drive time (DT) to specialists and higher area deprivation index (ADI) are linked to worse outcomes in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but their impact on postil...

item.title: Crohn's Disease Complicated by Undiagnosed Hemophilia A: A Case of Recurrent Massive Lower Gastrointestinal Bleeding.
item.description: ACG case reports journal - Case report - Severe gastrointestinal bleeding is a rare but potentially fatal complication of Crohn's disease (CD). In the literature, there are some previously described cases, but there are no clear guideline...

item.title: VALIDATE-PERIANAL: an international real-world multi-centre exploratory validation of the TOpClass definition of a radiologically healed fistula in perianal fistulising Crohn's disease.
item.description: Insights into imaging - Radiological healing on MRI is the ultimate therapeutic goal in Class 2a perianal fistulising Crohn's disease (pfCD). The TOpClass consortium recently defined radiological healing (TOpClass-RH) by ...

item.title: Primary Intestinal Lymphangiectasia Presenting as Recurrent Chylous Ascites: A Rare Case.
item.description: Journal of investigative medicine high impact case reports - Case report - Primary intestinal lymphangiectasia (PIL) is a rare protein-losing enteropathy, typically diagnosed in childhood. Adult-onset PIL is exceptionally rare and poses a significant diagnostic challenge,...

item.title: Prognostic Utility of Albumin and the C-Reactive Protein-to-Albumin Ratio for Predicting Intravenous Steroid Response in Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC) is a high-risk condition associated with intense inflammation and a substantial risk of early colectomy. Intravenous steroids (IVS) rem...

item.title: Evaluation of Long-Term Outcomes of Crohn's Disease Complicated by Intra-Abdominal Abscess: A Retrospective International Cohort Study.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background: Crohn's disease complicated by intra-abdominal abscesses often requires surgery. Percutaneous drainage may prevent surgery, but optimal post-drainage management is unclear. We aimed to ...

item.title: Self-Assembly of Anti-Inflammatory Peptide Amphiphiles for Mucosal Health.
item.description: Chembiochem : a European journal of chemical biology - Broad spectrum anti-inflammatory peptide amphiphiles (AIF-PAs) have been developed as an innovative strategy for the treatment of chronic mucosal inflammation and the prevention of HIV transmission...

item.title: Impact of Delayed Diagnosis in IBD on Clinical Outcomes and Healthcare Delivery.
item.description: Diagnostics (Basel, Switzerland) - Background: Delays in diagnosis are unfortunately quite common in most health systems. It is apparent that timely diagnosis is more likely to have a favourable outcome. However, there may be many r...

item.title: Dietary Management After Ulcerative Colitis Surgery: A Thematic Analysis of TikTok Content.
item.description: Nutrients - Background/Objectives: For patients with Ulcerative Colitis (UC) requiring surgical treatment, post-operative dietary management can pose significant challenges. TikTok is emerging as a popular soc...
